Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Bodo (code=brx, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=30. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Baksa.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Bodo.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 30
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Hawker
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: Assam
- district: Baksa
- first_language: Bodo
- clinical_persona_summary: A 30-year-old female hawker from Baksa, Assam, with middle education, currently married, and fluent in Bodo and Assamese. She works in a rural market, managing inventory and customer interactions while balancing community responsibilities. Her lifestyle includes early-morning walks and yoga, with a preference for local cultural activities and community-oriented work.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

Patient presented for routine antenatal checkup at 28 weeks gestation. Blood pressure 110/70 mmHg, weight 65 kg. Fundal height 28 cm. Fetal heart rate 145 bpm. Urine protein negative. Blood sugar fasting 85 mg/dL. Hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL. Ultrasound shows normal fetal growth with breech presentation. Patient advised for external cephalic version at 32 weeks. Previous delivery history: one normal vaginal delivery in 2018. No complications reported. Prescribed iron folic acid tablets, calcium supplements. Tetanus toxoid injection administered. Next appointment scheduled for 4 weeks later. Postnatal care plan discussed. Delivery expected in March 2025. Insurance coverage verified for normal delivery expenses. TPA authorization requested for planned cesarean section due to breech presentation.

payload.professional_persona: An organized elder named Rajni Das coordinates community festivals, manages household budgeting with meticulous care, and they mentor younger women in traditional weaving and herbal remedies.
payload.linguistic_persona: An oral multilingual named Rajni Das speaks Bodo fluently, understands Assamese and Bengali, and they rely on family members for any written communication.
payload.sports_persona: An enthusiastic community member named Rajni Das enjoys playing Ludo with grandchildren, takes daily walks along the Brahmaputra riverbank, and they cheer for the Assam cricket team and NorthEast United FC during televised matches.
payload.arts_persona: An expressive elder named Rajni Das sings traditional folk lullabies, listens to classic Bollywood films starring Amitabh Bachchan, and they relish the soulful music of Bhupen Hazarika during quiet evenings.
payload.travel_persona: An adventure‑in‑spirit elder named Rajni Das travels by bus to the nearby Tezpur market, visits Guwahati for temple festivals, and they dream of a pilgrimage to the Sikkim monasteries one day.
payload.culinary_persona: An accomplished cook named Rajni Das prepares onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi using mustard oil, bamboo shoots, and fresh herbs, and they delight guests with these dishes during Bwisagu and family gatherings.
payload.persona: Rajni Das blends routine devotion, community stewardship, and cultural storytelling, and they juggle modest finances, health quirks, and a love for classic Bollywood cinema.
payload.cultural_background: Rajni Das is a member of the Bodo community, a recognised Scheduled Tribe in Assam. She lives in the rural hinterland of Sonitpur, where agrarian life and the Brahmaputra floodplains shape daily routines. Her cultural world blends Bodo tribal customs—such as the Bwisagu New Year celebration, traditional weaving (gamosa), folk music, and reverence for nature spirits—with mainstream Assamese Hindu practices, including temple worship and festivals like Durga Puja. The close‑knit village setting fosters strong communal bonds, inter‑generational respect, and adherence to age‑old rituals that honour the cycles of the seasons and agricultural harvests.
payload.linguistic_background: Rajni’s first language is Bodo, which she speaks fluently but cannot read or write due to her lack of formal schooling. She learned Assamese as a second language to communicate with neighbours, buy groceries, and attend the local market; she understands spoken Assamese well and can follow basic oral instructions, though she is not literate in its script. Her third language, Bengali, is acquired through listening to Bengali families nearby and occasional television programmes; she can comprehend simple conversations. All three languages are used primarily in oral contexts, and she relies on family members or neighbours for any written communication such as notices, medication labels or bank slips.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Rajni is skilled in managing a large household with limited resources, a talent honed over decades of homemaking. She excels at traditional Bodo cooking, preparing dishes such as onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi. Her weaving abilities allow her to produce bamboo mats and gamosa cloths for everyday use and festive occasions. She coordinates community events, especially festivals like Bwisagu and Durga Puja, ensuring smooth logistics and hospitality. Rajni possesses practical knowledge of herbal remedies and natural medicine, often preparing simple treatments for common ailments. She handles household budgeting with careful jugaad, stretching the family’s modest income to meet all needs. Additionally, she provides reliable childcare and eldercare, and is an engaging oral storyteller, preserving local folktales for younger generations.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: [ "Household management", "Traditional Bodo cooking", "Weaving (bamboo mats & gamosa)", "Community event coordination", "Herbal remedy knowledge", "Budgeting with jugaad", "Childcare and eldercare", "Oral storytelling", "Hospitality and hosting" ]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Rajni finds joy in singing traditional Bodo folk songs and lullabies, weaving bamboo mats and gamosa cloths, and tending a small kitchen garden of vegetables and herbs. She loves preparing festive dishes for occasions like Bwisagu and Durga Puja, and looks forward to community melas where she can chat with neighbours. Storytelling is a favorite pastime; she often narrates local legends to her grandchildren. Rajni also participates actively in family puja and village religious ceremonies. In quieter moments, she enjoys listening to radio programs, playing simple board games like Ludo with the kids, and watching classic Bollywood movies.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: [ "Singing Bodo folk songs", "Weaving bamboo mats & gamosa", "Gardening (vegetables & herbs)", "Cooking festive dishes", "Attending village melas", "Storytelling", "Participating in puja ceremonies", "Playing board games (Ludo) with grandchildren", "Watching classic Bollywood movies", "Listening to radio programs" ]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Rajni’s main ambition is to nurture the younger generation by passing on Bodo cultural knowledge, traditional recipes, and ethical values. She hopes to see her grandchildren complete higher education and secure stable livelihoods. Though she has no formal career aspirations, she wishes to remain active in the village by helping organize festivals, mentoring other women in household management and traditional crafts, and offering guidance in family matters. Maintaining her health so she can support her husband and continue her role as a respected elder in the community is also a priority.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 71-year-old female from Assam’s Sonitpur district, currently married, with no formal occupation and an illiterate education level. She speaks Bodo as her first language and uses Assamese for daily communication, living in a rural setting. Clinically, she manages modest finances, has health quirks, and engages in routine activities like daily walks and community involvement.

- sex / gender: Female
- age: 71
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Assam
- district: Sonitpur
- first_language: Bodo
- clinical_persona_summary: A 71-year-old female from Assam’s Sonitpur district, currently married, with no formal occupation and an illiterate education level. She speaks Bodo as her first language and uses Assamese for daily communication, living in a rural setting. Clinically, she manages modest finances, has health quirks, and engages in routine activities like daily walks and community involvement.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]

Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]

- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]

payload.professional_persona: An organized elder named Rajni Das coordinates community festivals, manages household budgeting with meticulous care, and they mentor younger women in traditional weaving and herbal remedies.
payload.linguistic_persona: An oral multilingual named Rajni Das speaks Bodo fluently, understands Assamese and Bengali, and they rely on family members for any written communication.
payload.sports_persona: An enthusiastic community member named Rajni Das enjoys playing Ludo with grandchildren, takes daily walks along the Brahmaputra riverbank, and they cheer for the Assam cricket team and NorthEast United FC during televised matches.
payload.arts_persona: An expressive elder named Rajni Das sings traditional folk lullabies, listens to classic Bollywood films starring Amitabh Bachchan, and they relish the soulful music of Bhupen Hazarika during quiet evenings.
payload.travel_persona: An adventure‑in‑spirit elder named Rajni Das travels by bus to the nearby Tezpur market, visits Guwahati for temple festivals, and they dream of a pilgrimage to the Sikkim monasteries one day.
payload.culinary_persona: An accomplished cook named Rajni Das prepares onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi using mustard oil, bamboo shoots, and fresh herbs, and they delight guests with these dishes during Bwisagu and family gatherings.
payload.persona: Rajni Das blends routine devotion, community stewardship, and cultural storytelling, and they juggle modest finances, health quirks, and a love for classic Bollywood cinema.
payload.cultural_background: Rajni Das is a member of the Bodo community, a recognised Scheduled Tribe in Assam. She lives in the rural hinterland of Sonitpur, where agrarian life and the Brahmaputra floodplains shape daily routines. Her cultural world blends Bodo tribal customs—such as the Bwisagu New Year celebration, traditional weaving (gamosa), folk music, and reverence for nature spirits—with mainstream Assamese Hindu practices, including temple worship and festivals like Durga Puja. The close‑knit village setting fosters strong communal bonds, inter‑generational respect, and adherence to age‑old rituals that honour the cycles of the seasons and agricultural harvests.
payload.linguistic_background: Rajni’s first language is Bodo, which she speaks fluently but cannot read or write due to her lack of formal schooling. She learned Assamese as a second language to communicate with neighbours, buy groceries, and attend the local market; she understands spoken Assamese well and can follow basic oral instructions, though she is not literate in its script. Her third language, Bengali, is acquired through listening to Bengali families nearby and occasional television programmes; she can comprehend simple conversations. All three languages are used primarily in oral contexts, and she relies on family members or neighbours for any written communication such as notices, medication labels or bank slips.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Rajni is skilled in managing a large household with limited resources, a talent honed over decades of homemaking. She excels at traditional Bodo cooking, preparing dishes such as onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi. Her weaving abilities allow her to produce bamboo mats and gamosa cloths for everyday use and festive occasions. She coordinates community events, especially festivals like Bwisagu and Durga Puja, ensuring smooth logistics and hospitality. Rajni possesses practical knowledge of herbal remedies and natural medicine, often preparing simple treatments for common ailments. She handles household budgeting with careful jugaad, stretching the family’s modest income to meet all needs. Additionally, she provides reliable childcare and eldercare, and is an engaging oral storyteller, preserving local folktales for younger generations.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: [ "Household management", "Traditional Bodo cooking", "Weaving (bamboo mats & gamosa)", "Community event coordination", "Herbal remedy knowledge", "Budgeting with jugaad", "Childcare and eldercare", "Oral storytelling", "Hospitality and hosting" ]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Rajni finds joy in singing traditional Bodo folk songs and lullabies, weaving bamboo mats and gamosa cloths, and tending a small kitchen garden of vegetables and herbs. She loves preparing festive dishes for occasions like Bwisagu and Durga Puja, and looks forward to community melas where she can chat with neighbours. Storytelling is a favorite pastime; she often narrates local legends to her grandchildren. Rajni also participates actively in family puja and village religious ceremonies. In quieter moments, she enjoys listening to radio programs, playing simple board games like Ludo with the kids, and watching classic Bollywood movies.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: [ "Singing Bodo folk songs", "Weaving bamboo mats & gamosa", "Gardening (vegetables & herbs)", "Cooking festive dishes", "Attending village melas", "Storytelling", "Participating in puja ceremonies", "Playing board games (Ludo) with grandchildren", "Watching classic Bollywood movies", "Listening to radio programs" ]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Rajni’s main ambition is to nurture the younger generation by passing on Bodo cultural knowledge, traditional recipes, and ethical values. She hopes to see her grandchildren complete higher education and secure stable livelihoods. Though she has no formal career aspirations, she wishes to remain active in the village by helping organize festivals, mentoring other women in household management and traditional crafts, and offering guidance in family matters. Maintaining her health so she can support her husband and continue her role as a respected elder in the community is also a priority.

Advise dietary modifications for cholesterol. Prescribe iron supplements for anemia. Follow-up in 3 months.

payload.arts_persona: An expressive elder named Rajni Das sings traditional folk lullabies, listens to classic Bollywood films starring Amitabh Bachchan, and they relish the soulful music of Bhupen Hazarika during quiet evenings.
payload.travel_persona: An adventure‑in‑spirit elder named Rajni Das travels by bus to the nearby Tezpur market, visits Guwahati for temple festivals, and they dream of a pilgrimage to the Sikkim monasteries one day.
payload.culinary_persona: An accomplished cook named Rajni Das prepares onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi using mustard oil, bamboo shoots, and fresh herbs, and they delight guests with these dishes during Bwisagu and family gatherings.
payload.persona: Rajni Das blends routine devotion, community stewardship, and cultural storytelling, and they juggle modest finances, health quirks, and a love for classic Bollywood cinema.
payload.cultural_background: Rajni Das is a member of the Bodo community, a recognised Scheduled Tribe in Assam. She lives in the rural hinterland of Sonitpur, where agrarian life and the Brahmaputra floodplains shape daily routines. Her cultural world blends Bodo tribal customs—such as the Bwisagu New Year celebration, traditional weaving (gamosa), folk music, and reverence for nature spirits—with mainstream Assamese Hindu practices, including temple worship and festivals like Durga Puja. The close‑knit village setting fosters strong communal bonds, inter‑generational respect, and adherence to age‑old rituals that honour the cycles of the seasons and agricultural harvests.
payload.linguistic_background: Rajni’s first language is Bodo, which she speaks fluently but cannot read or write due to her lack of formal schooling. She learned Assamese as a second language to communicate with neighbours, buy groceries, and attend the local market; she understands spoken Assamese well and can follow basic oral instructions, though she is not literate in its script. Her third language, Bengali, is acquired through listening to Bengali families nearby and occasional television programmes; she can comprehend simple conversations. All three languages are used primarily in oral contexts, and she relies on family members or neighbours for any written communication such as notices, medication labels or bank slips.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Rajni is skilled in managing a large household with limited resources, a talent honed over decades of homemaking. She excels at traditional Bodo cooking, preparing dishes such as onla, mitha madua, and sukhmi. Her weaving abilities allow her to produce bamboo mats and gamosa cloths for everyday use and festive occasions. She coordinates community events, especially festivals like Bwisagu and Durga Puja, ensuring smooth logistics and hospitality. Rajni possesses practical knowledge of herbal remedies and natural medicine, often preparing simple treatments for common ailments. She handles household budgeting with careful jugaad, stretching the family’s modest income to meet all needs. Additionally, she provides reliable childcare and eldercare, and is an engaging oral storyteller, preserving local folktales for younger generations.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Bodo (code=brx, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Assam, district=Sonitpur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Bodo.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 71
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Assam
- district: Sonitpur
- first_language: Bodo
- clinical_persona_summary: A 71-year-old female from Assam's Sonitpur district, currently married and retired, with a Bodo first language and limited formal education. She lives in a rural setting, managing household finances and community activities while navigating modest health challenges. Her lifestyle includes daily walks, traditional cooking, and engagement with local cultural practices.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Sonitpur District Hospital]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Bina Boro]], [[AGE|71]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajiv Sharma]]
Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
Patient ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Sonitpur]]
Address: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|Village: Gohpur, P.O. Gohpur, Sonitpur, Assam 784101]]

Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

Rx:
- Isoniazid 300mg - 1 tablet daily for 6 months
- Rifampicin 450mg - 1 tablet daily for 6 months  
- Pyrazinamide 750mg - 1 tablet daily for 2 months
- Ethambutol 800mg - 1 tablet daily for 2 months
- Metformin 500mg - 1 tablet twice daily for diabetes

Follow-up: Review in 2 weeks for sputum examination and blood sugar monitoring. Continue DOTS therapy as per Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme guidelines.

[[ABHA_ADDRESS|bina.boro@abdm]]

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Kathua.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Police Officers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Kathua
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: Anuradha Devi is a 38-year-old female police officer in rural Kathua, Jammu & Kashmir, married, with a Dogri-speaking background and limited formal education. She is illiterate but functionally conversant in spoken Hindi and English, and her lifestyle includes community-focused work, physical fitness, and cultural engagement with regional festivals. Her clinical presentation may be influenced by limited literacy and potential health behaviors related to dietary habits and stress management.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kathua]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-JK-001]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anuradha Devi]] | DOB [[DOB|1986-05-22]] | Age: [[AGE|38]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Police Officer]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9419012345]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-2024-0815]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Kathua]]

Chief complaint: Patient presents with persistent anxiety, difficulty sleeping, and intrusive thoughts for the past 3 months. Reports increased irritability at work and difficulty concentrating during duty hours.

History of present illness: Mrs. Devi reports symptoms began after a stressful transfer to a new posting. She experiences racing thoughts, palpitations, and excessive worry about family safety. Sleep onset latency has increased to 2-3 hours. Reports occasional panic attacks with sweating and trembling.

Past psychiatric history: No previous psychiatric treatment. No history of depression or anxiety disorders.

Medical history: Hypertension diagnosed 2 years ago, controlled with medication. No known allergies.

Medications: Amlodipine 5mg daily, prescribed by local physician.

Family history: Mother has hypertension. No known psychiatric disorders in family.

Social history: Married with two children. Lives in rural Kathua. Reports high stress due to job demands and family responsibilities. Limited formal education but functionally literate in Hindi.

Mental status examination: Alert and oriented x4. Speech normal rate and rhythm. Mood anxious, affect constricted. Thought process logical, no delusions or hallucinations. Insight fair, judgment impaired due to anxiety.

Assessment: 1. Generalized Anxiety Disorder 2. Insomnia 3. Hypertension

Plan: 1. Start Sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety 2. Refer to sleep clinic for insomnia management 3. Continue antihypertensive medication 4. Provide stress management techniques 5. Follow up in 2 weeks. Counsel on importance of regular medication and lifestyle modifications including regular exercise and adequate sleep hygiene.

payload.culinary_persona: Anuradha Devi loves cooking authentic Jammu dishes such as rajma-chawal, kalari cheese fry, and sweet lal puri, favoring fresh coriander, ginger, and dried fenugreek in their recipes, while they also enjoy indulging in street snacks like samosa during village fairs.
payload.persona: Anuradha Devi is a diligent 38‑year‑old police officer who blends disciplined structure with an outgoing love for folk music, Kabaddi, and community safety, while they quietly battle illiteracy and a habit of splurging on festive sweets.
payload.cultural_background: Anuradha belongs to the Dogri‑speaking Dogra community of Kathua in the Jammu region of Jammu & Kashmir. Her family follows traditional Hindu customs, celebrates regional festivals such as Lohri, Baisakhi and Navratri, and participates in community gatherings at the local mandir. Growing up in a rural setting has ingrained a strong sense of kinship, respect for agrarian life, and practical values rooted in Dogri culture.
payload.linguistic_background: Anuradha is fluent in spoken Dogri and can comfortably converse in her mother tongue for everyday interactions. Due to her illiteracy, she cannot read or write in Dogri, Hindi, or English, but she has a functional grasp of spoken Hindi, which is commonly used in the region, enabling basic communication with officials and neighbours.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Anuradha possesses practical law‑enforcement skills honed through years of service as a police officer in a rural setting. She is adept at community policing, handling local disputes, conducting routine patrols, and providing first‑aid assistance. Her strong physical fitness, quick decision‑making, and ability to negotiate calmly under pressure make her an effective crisis‑manager. She also has deep knowledge of the local geography, Dogri customs, and cultural sensitivities, which aids in building trust with villagers.

payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Kathua.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Police Officers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Kathua
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: Anuradha Devi is a 38-year-old female police officer in rural Kathua, Jammu & Kashmir, currently married, with a Dogri-speaking background and a rural education level. She is illiterate but functionally conversant in spoken Hindi and English, and her lifestyle includes community-oriented activities and a habit of spending on festive sweets. Her professional role involves community policing and first-aid, while her cultural context includes traditional Hindu customs and regional festivals.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

[[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
[[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]] [[CVV|456]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9419234567]] [[PIN|1850]]

Patient Anuradha Devi, a 38-year-old female police officer from rural Kathua, presented for high-risk antenatal care at 32 weeks gestation. She reported decreased fetal movements and mild abdominal discomfort. Examination revealed BP 140/90 mmHg, fundal height 34 cm, and fetal heart rate 145 bpm. Ultrasound confirmed singleton pregnancy with oligohydramnios. Patient was admitted for observation and received IV fluids, antihypertensive medication (labetalol 100mg), and betamethasone for fetal lung maturity. She delivered a healthy male infant weighing 2.8 kg via spontaneous vaginal delivery after 48 hours of induction. Postpartum period was complicated by mild postpartum hemorrhage managed with oxytocin infusion. Patient was discharged on day 5 with iron supplements and advised for postnatal follow-up. Total hospital bill amount: ₹45,000 for delivery and antenatal care.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

- sex / gender: Female
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Jammu
- first_language: Dogri

ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]

ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Dogri (code=doi, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Jammu & Kashmir, district=Jammu.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Dogri.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Jammu & Kashmir
- district: Jammu
- first_language: Dogri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 48-year-old female from Jammu & Kashmir, currently married, with a matriculation education, works as a homemaker. She is fluent in Dogri and Hindi, living in an urban setting. Her lifestyle includes a competitive drive and a cautious approach, with a diet rich in traditional Kashmiri foods.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
MUST tag [[APPOINTMENT_ID|…]] whenever an appointment is mentioned.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text: Dear [[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Devi]], your General Medicine appointment [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240720-03]] is confirmed for 20-July at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SMGS Hospital Jammu]] with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]. Please bring your MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] and arrive 15 minutes early. Contact [[PHONE_NUMBER|9419234567]] for queries.

payload.sports_persona: Fatma Shamar enjoys morning walks in their garden, follows the Jammu & Kashmir cricket team and the Kolkata Knight Riders with a competitive spirit, and occasionally practices light yoga to alleviate joint stiffness, though they remain skeptical of overly commercial fitness trends.
payload.arts_persona: Fatma Shamar showcases artistic flair through intricate Kashmiri phulkari and regional stitch work, listens avidly to folk singers like K. S. Chahar while watching classic Bollywood films such as Sholay and enjoying the performances of Amitabh Bachchan, reflecting their curiosity and meticulous eye for detail.
payload.travel_persona: Fatma Shamar enjoys carefully planned trips to nearby hill stations like Gulmarg and Pahalgam, cherishes family outings to Delhi's historic sites, and they dream of exploring the lakes of Kashmir, always budgeting each journey to balance adventure with financial prudence.
payload.culinary_persona: Fatma Shamar delights in tasting innovative dishes at local restaurants while mastering traditional recipes like saffron‑infused 'kashmiri noor' and wheat flatbread served with mustard oil, favoring ingredients such as dried apricots, almonds, and fresh garden herbs, yet they often hesitate to replicate complex restaurant flavours at home.
payload.persona: Fatma Shamar blends meticulous organization, a competitive drive, and a curiosity for new experiences with a lingering worry that fuels their diligent yet sometimes overly cautious approach to life.
payload.cultural_background: Fatma belongs to the Dogra Muslim community of Jammu, an urban setting where Dogri language and Kashmiri cultural influences intermix with Islamic traditions. She grew up celebrating Eid and local festivals such as Lohri, and maintains a household that reflects both Dogra hospitality and modest Muslim values.
payload.linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Dogri, which she speaks fluently, reads and writes in the Devanagari script. She is also proficient in Hindi, using it for daily interactions, official matters, and education. With only matric qualification, her English is limited to basic comprehension. She can understand and recite Urdu in religious contexts, though not listed as a formal language.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Fatma excels in household management, including budgeting, meal planning, and organizing family events. She is adept at cooking traditional Dogri and Kashmiri dishes, especially festive sweets like 'kashmiri noor' and 'dogri roti'. Her embroidery skills, particularly Kashmiri phulkari and Dogri stitch work, are well-regarded in her community. She also has experience in guiding her children’s schoolwork and assisting neighbours with basic financial advice, reflecting her meticulous and reliable nature.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Household Management', 'Budgeting', 'Meal Planning', 'Traditional Dogri Cooking', 'Kashmiri Sweet Preparation', 'Embroidery (Phulkari & Dogri Stitch)', 'Children’s Educational Support', 'Basic Financial Guidance']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys experimenting with new restaurant dishes while preserving her own traditional recipes, spending time on embroidery projects, tending to a small vegetable garden, reading Quranic commentary, and participating in local women’s self‑help groups. On weekends, she often listens to Dogri folk music and watches classic Bollywood films, balancing solitude with occasional social gatherings.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Experimenting with New Recipes', 'Embroidery Projects', 'Vegetable Gardening', 'Reading Quranic Commentary', 'Women’s Self‑Help Group Participation', 'Listening to Dogri Folk Music', 'Watching Classic Bollywood Movies']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired from formal work, Fatma aims to deepen her involvement in community service, especially by teaching traditional cooking and embroidery to younger women. She aspires to document Dogri family recipes and stories for future generations, hoping to publish a modest community cookbook. Additionally, she wishes to ensure her children’s grandchildren pursue quality education and maintain the family’s financial stability.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 48-year-old female from Jammu & Kashmir, currently married, with a matriculation education and living in an urban setting. She speaks Dogri and Hindi, and her lifestyle includes morning walks, watching cricket, and practicing light yoga for joint stiffness. Her cultural background is Dogra Muslim, and she enjoys traditional cooking and embroidery.

Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).

The appendix is visualized and appears normal. The pancreas, gallbladder, and biliary tree are unremarkable. No evidence of bowel obstruction. The findings are consistent with a complex ovarian neoplasm, likely a borderline tumor or cystadenocarcinoma. Surgical consultation recommended for definitive management.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]

  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]

  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
