payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
MATERNAL SAFETY: clinical content must be age-plausible for persona age=32. Routine ANC/delivery typically ages 18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC/PNC/contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy as a routine story.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Thiruvananthapuram.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Labourer, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Thiruvananthapuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: Megha John is a 32-year-old female labourer from a rural area in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, who is married and holds a graduate degree. She speaks Malayalam and functional English, and works in construction and agriculture. Her lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional yoga, with no significant sports or travel history.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Megha John]] [[AGE|32]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-2024-0321-001]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|megha.john@example.com]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram]]
--- chat ---
Patient: Namaskaram Doctor, I'm Megha John, 32 years old, 28 weeks pregnant. I'm having some swelling in my feet and hands since yesterday. Also feeling very tired and having mild headaches. Can you please advise?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Namaskaram Megha. I understand your concern. Swelling in feet and hands at 28 weeks is common but we need to monitor it carefully. Please check your blood pressure at home if you have a machine. For the headaches, try to rest in a dark room and drink plenty of water. Avoid standing for long periods.

Patient: I don't have a BP machine at home Doctor. My husband works in construction and we're from a rural area. We don't have much money for extra tests.

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: I understand your situation Megha. Please come to the hospital tomorrow morning for a check-up. The hospital will provide free BP monitoring and basic tests. If you notice severe swelling, sudden weight gain, or vision changes, please come immediately or call emergency.

Patient: Thank you Doctor. What about my diet? I'm eating rice, fish curry, and vegetables. Is that enough?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Your diet sounds good Megha. Continue eating fish, eggs, and leafy vegetables. Add more fruits like papaya and banana. Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily. Avoid processed foods and excess salt. Take your iron and calcium tablets regularly.

Patient: I'm taking the tablets given at the last ANC visit. When should I come for the next appointment?

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: Your next appointment is scheduled for 32 weeks, which is in 4 weeks. Please come to the hospital OPD on Tuesday morning. If you have any concerns before then, you can message me through the portal or call the hospital reception.

Patient: Thank you so much Doctor. You've been very helpful.

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Nair]]: You're welcome Megha. Take care and take rest. We'll see you in 4 weeks.

She grew up amidst the coconut groves and backwaters, celebrating Onam with her family while also observing Christmas and Easter with her church community. Her cultural identity blends the Kerala coastal lifestyle, the communal values of village life, and the traditions of the Saint Thomas Christian community, such as attending weekly mass, participating in church feasts, and enjoying Malayalam folk songs and dance.
payload.linguistic_background: Megha's mother tongue is Malayalam, and she is fully literate in reading and writing Malayalam. She completed a graduate degree in arts, which required proficiency in English, so she can understand, read and write English at a functional level for official documents and basic communication. She does not have a second or third language, though she can follow simple Hindi phrases learned in school.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Megha has practical skills acquired through years of labour work in construction and agriculture, including masonry, carpentry basics, irrigation maintenance, and planting. She is adept at organising community tasks, coordinating groups for festivals and local development projects, and maintains basic bookkeeping for small co‑ops. Her graduate education equips her with good reading, writing, and communication abilities in English and Malayalam, allowing her to manage paperwork and liaise with supervisors. Additionally, she is proficient in using simple digital tools like mobile phones for messaging and basic internet browsing.

Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Institute of Medical Sciences, Imphal]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Nongthombam Bimol]] DOB [[DOB|1945-08-22]] [[AGE|79]] [[GENDER|Female]]
[[MRN|IMPH-2024-0815-001]] [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-IMPH-001]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|04]]
[[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Ibohal Singh]]
[[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]

Admission Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis with severe anemia and hypertensive crisis.
History of Present Illness: The patient, a 79-year-old widowed homemaker from Imphal East, presented with a three-week history of productive cough, intermittent low-grade fever, and profound weight loss of approximately 5 kg. She has a known history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus, which she reports are not well-controlled at home. On examination, she was cachectic, pale, and febrile with blood pressure recorded at 180/110 mmHg and random blood sugar of 280 mg/dL.

Investigations: Sputum smear microscopy was positive for Acid-Fast Bacilli (AFB). Chest X-ray revealed a cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe. Complete blood count showed hemoglobin of 6.8 g/dL. Renal and liver function tests were within acceptable limits.

Course in Hospital: The patient was initiated on Category I Anti-Tubercular Therapy (ATT) under Directly Observed Treatment Short-course (DOTS). Her hypertensive emergency was managed with intravenous labetalol and later transitioned to oral antihypertensives. Blood sugar was controlled with a sliding scale of insulin and oral hypoglycemic agents. She received two units of packed red blood cells for symptomatic anemia. Over the next 21 days, she remained afebrile, her cough frequency reduced significantly, and her appetite improved. She was hemodynamically stable on oral medications.

Discharge Condition: The patient is clinically stable and afebrile for the last 72 hours. Cough has markedly improved, and she reports increased energy levels. She is hemodynamically stable on oral antihypertensive and antidiabetic medications.

Discharge Advice: The patient is discharged with a total of six months of Anti-Tubercular Therapy, with three months remaining to be completed as DOTS. She is advised to continue her antihypertensive and antidiabetic medications regularly. A high-protein, low-salt, and low-sugar diet is recommended. She is scheduled for a follow-up at the TB clinic in one week and the Medicine OPD in two weeks. Her son, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Nongthombam Ratan]], has been thoroughly counseled regarding medication adherence, potential side effects of ATT, and the importance of follow-up visits. He was provided with the patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9435123456]] for any emergencies. The patient's [[ABHA_ADDRESS|nongthombam.bimol@abdm]] has been updated in the hospital records. The patient belongs to the [[CASTE|Meitei]] community.

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 79
- marital_status: Widowed
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 79-year-old female, widowed, retired homemaker from Imphal East, Manipur, with an illiterate education level, who speaks Manipuri as her first language and basic English. She lives in an urban zone and maintains a traditional lifestyle, preserving cultural stories and rituals. Her daily routine includes gentle walks and participation in community activities.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Hospital Imphal]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Thounaojam Singh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Chanu Devi]], [[AGE|79]] / [[GENDER|Female]], District [[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
Reason: Evaluation of uncontrolled hypertension and recurrent episodes of dizziness

Dear Colleague,

This is to refer [[PATIENT_NAME|Chanu Devi]], a 79-year-old female patient from Imphal East, Manipur, who presents with a history of hypertension for the past 15 years. The patient reports episodes of dizziness and occasional headaches over the last two months despite regular medication.

On examination, her blood pressure was recorded at 160/95 mmHg. She has mild pedal edema and her pulse rate is 88 beats per minute. Laboratory investigations show fasting blood glucose of 145 mg/dL and serum creatinine of 1.8 mg/dL.

The patient is currently on Amlodipine 5mg daily and Metformin 500mg twice daily. She has no known allergies and is not on any other medications. Her [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] is attached for your reference.

For further evaluation and management, please arrange for cardiac evaluation including ECG and echocardiography. The patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] is available for coordination.

The patient's [[RELATIVE_NAME|Th. Ibocha Devi]] (daughter) will accompany her for the consultation. Her [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|M1234567]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|ML-01-2010-0012345]] are available for identification purposes.

Thank you for your attention to this case.

Sincerely,
[[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Thounaojam Singh]]
General Medicine
Regional Hospital Imphal

payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 79
- marital_status: Widowed
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 79-year-old female, widowed, retired homemaker from Imphal East, Manipur, with an illiterate education level, speaks Manipuri as her first language and has limited English proficiency. She resides in an urban zone and maintains a traditional lifestyle, preserving folk stories and rituals. Clinically, she presents with occasional anxiety and forgetfulness, which may impact medication adherence. Her daily routine includes gentle morning walks and participation in community cultural activities.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Radiology report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Hospital Imphal]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Chanu Devi]] [[AGE|79]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Singh]]
Findings: Plain abdominal radiograph reveals multiple radio-opaque calculi in the right upper quadrant measuring approximately 2.5cm and 1.8cm respectively. No evidence of bowel obstruction or free air under the diaphragm. The liver silhouette appears normal in size and contour. Gallbladder fossa is not clearly visualized. No calcified lymph nodes identified. Soft tissue planes are preserved. No acute osseous abnormalities noted.

Report: The findings are consistent with cholelithiasis. Surgical consultation recommended for possible cholecystectomy given the size and number of stones. No evidence of acute surgical emergency at this time.

[[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9412345678]]
[[HOSPITAL_ID|RH-IMPHAL-001]]
[[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

payload.professional_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a meticulous ordained priest and cultural program manager, blends ancient ritual expertise with razor-sharp project planning to run community festivals, workshops and heritage preservation initiatives, while their innate curiosity and sociable nature keep them constantly seeking innovative ways to engage youth, even as they wrestle with occasional bouts of worry over flawless execution.
payload.linguistic_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, fluent in Manipuri (both spoken and the Meitei Mayek script), commands polished English for scholarly discourse, and maintains functional Hindi literacy for everyday interactions, allowing them to translate sacred texts and bridge generational communication gaps.
payload.sports_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an avid football fan, regularly watches NEROCA FC matches, plays weekly badminton at the Imphal East community centre, and joins local marathon runs during the cooler winter months, balancing energetic activity with occasional bouts of anxiety about performance.
payload.arts_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a passionate patron of Manipuri arts, frequently attends Pena concerts by renowned maestro Kh. Mangang Singh, watches contemporary theatre productions at the Sangeet Natak Bhavan, and occasionally performs Ras Lila dances with local youth groups, drawing inspiration from artists like Seema Khaidem and filmmaker Aribam Syam Sharma.
payload.travel_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, an exploratory traveler, prefers short, well-planned road trips to nearby hill stations like Shillong and Cherrapunji, craves cultural immersion in historic places such as Varanasi and Bodh Gaya, and dreams of a pilgrimage to the Himalayan monasteries of Ladakh, always budgeting trips carefully to accommodate community responsibilities.
payload.culinary_persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a connoisseur of Manipuri cuisine, delights in the fiery tang of Eromba made with boiled vegetables and ngari (fermented fish), savors the fresh crunch of Singju with lotus stem and bamboo shoots, and often treats friends to a steaming bowl of Chak-hao (black rice) pulao seasoned with cumin and mustard leaves, though they rarely cook at home, preferring to explore street stalls.
payload.persona: Heikrujam Lhouvum, a disciplined yet curious cultural priest, balances meticulous community projects with a love for arts, wrestles with frequent worries, and never says no to a good cup of tea.
payload.cultural_background: Heikrujam Lhouvum hails from Imphal East, an urban area of Manipur, and belongs to the Meitei (Manipuri) community. His upbringing was steeped in the rich cultural tapestry of Manipur, where traditional dance forms such as Ras Lila, the classical music of the Pena, and communal festivals like Lai Haraoba are integral parts of daily life. Growing up in a city that blends ancient customs with contemporary influences, he has internalised a strong sense of communal responsibility, respect for elders, and an appreciation for artistic expression that reflects the syncretic heritage of the region.
payload.linguistic_background: He is a native Manipuri speaker, fluent in both spoken Manipuri and the Meitei Mayek script used for reading and writing. His secondary language is English, in which he is highly proficient across speaking, reading, and writing, enabling him to engage with broader academic and religious networks. Formal education through higher secondary level has also provided him with functional literacy in Hindi, though he primarily uses Manipuri and English for daily communication and professional duties.
payload.skills_and_expertise: Heikrujam possesses a blend of religious, cultural, and organizational competencies. As an ordained Sanamahi priest, he is skilled in conducting traditional rites, chanting sacred verses, and interpreting ancient myths. He excels in community outreach, counseling, and leading educational workshops on cultural heritage preservation. His disciplined nature translates into strong project management, event planning, and administrative abilities. Additionally, he has proficiency in the Manipuri language (Meitei Mayek script), English communication, and basic Hindi literacy, enabling effective translation and documentation of religious texts.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ['Traditional Sanamahi ritual conduct', 'Sacred chanting', 'Mythological interpretation', 'Community counseling', 'Cultural heritage preservation workshops', 'Project management', 'Event planning', 'Administrative coordination', 'Manipuri language proficiency (Meitei Mayek)', 'English communication', 'Basic Hindi literacy', 'Translation of religious texts']
payload.hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys attending and occasionally performing traditional Manipuri dance and music sessions, especially the Pena instrument. He is an avid reader of spiritual and philosophical texts, both in Manipuri and English. His curiosity leads him to explore contemporary art galleries and theatre productions in Imphal. He frequently organizes community gatherings, volunteers for local NGOs working on youth empowerment, and practices yoga and meditation to manage stress.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Traditional Manipuri dance', 'Playing the Pena instrument', 'Reading spiritual and philosophical literature', 'Visiting art galleries', 'Attending theatre productions', 'Organizing community gatherings', 'Volunteering with youth NGOs', 'Practicing yoga and meditation']
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: He aspires to strengthen the spiritual and cultural fabric of his community by establishing a dedicated Sanamahi cultural center in Imphal East that offers regular workshops on traditional arts, language, and rituals. He aims to train a new generation of lay religious facilitators, expand interfaith dialogue initiatives, and develop youth mentorship programs that integrate ethical teachings with modern education. Over the next decade, he envisions publishing a bilingual compendium of Sanamahi prayers and folklore, and positioning himself as a recognized thought leader in preserving Manipur’s intangible heritage.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 38-year-old male, currently married, works as an ordained religious worker in Manipur's Imphal East district. He holds higher secondary education and is fluent in Manipuri and English. His lifestyle includes frequent worry and a preference for tea.

Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.

- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.

Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-IMPH-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Institute of Medical Sciences, Imphal]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Singh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]], [[AGE|38]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Imphal East]]
Reason: Persistent cough with constitutional symptoms and associated hypertension requiring specialized evaluation

Dear Colleague,

This is to refer [[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]], a 38-year-old male patient residing in Imphal East for further evaluation and management of suspected pulmonary tuberculosis with comorbid hypertension.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]] presented with a 3-week history of productive cough, low-grade fever, night sweats, and weight loss of approximately 5 kg. The patient reports occasional hemoptysis and progressive dyspnea on exertion. Past medical history reveals newly diagnosed hypertension 6 months ago, currently managed with amlodipine 5mg daily. Family history is significant for diabetes mellitus in his father.

On examination, vital signs show BP 145/90 mmHg, pulse 98/min, respiratory rate 22/min, temperature 37.8°C. Chest examination reveals decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone with occasional rhonchi. Sputum examination shows numerous acid-fast bacilli on smear.

Given the clinical presentation and high TB burden in Manipur, I request your expertise for comprehensive management including drug susceptibility testing, evaluation of TB-diabetes interaction, and optimization of antihypertensive therapy.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Thokchom Sanjit Singh]] can be contacted at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9412345678]] and his [[RELATIVE_NAME|wife Thokchom Rina Devi]] at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. His [[MRN|MRN-IMPH-2024-0892]] and [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|A12345678]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|DL-MAN-2024-001234]] are available for reference.

Please coordinate with our team for smooth transition of care.

Sincerely,

payload.system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]

  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]

  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]

  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]

  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]

  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Manipuri (code=mni, script=Meitei). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Manipur, district=Imphal East.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Meitei for Manipuri.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Ordained Religious Workers, Other
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Manipur
- district: Imphal East
- first_language: Manipuri
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old male, currently married, works as an ordained religious worker in Manipur's Imphal East district. He holds higher secondary education and is a native Manipuri speaker, with English proficiency. His lifestyle includes frequent worry and a preference for tea, with no significant sports or culinary details.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Radiology Report — District Hospital Imphal | [[PATIENT_NAME|Thangjam Sanjit Singh]] [[AGE|38]] [[GENDER|Male]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. R. Singh]]
Findings: PA chest radiograph demonstrates clear lung fields bilaterally with no acute infiltrates or consolidations. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. Bony structures including ribs, clavicles, and thoracic spine appear unremarkable. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax identified. Diaphragms are well-visualized with normal contour.

[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MN-001]]
[[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
[[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Imphal]]

payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Dorje wishes to remain a dependable presence in his call‑centre for the next few years, gradually taking on a mentorship role where he can share his extensive experience with younger agents. Spiritually, he aspires to deepen his meditation practice and occasionally lead brief meditation sessions for community members, contributing to local welfare initiatives that promote health, education, and social harmony.

Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
payload.user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Hingoli.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 67
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Operator, Call Centre
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Hingoli
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 67-year-old male operator in a call centre, currently married, with no formal education and a rural background in Maharashtra's Hingoli district. His first language is Marathi, and he is illiterate in all languages. He lives in a close-knit rural community, where he follows a frugal lifestyle and has a history of financial and health concerns.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
payload.generated_text:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Hingoli]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Patil]] DOB [[DOB|1957-08-15]] [[AGE|67]] [[GENDER|Male]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0315]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|A1]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|08]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] | Course / advice: Patient presented with severe anxiety and depressive symptoms following recent financial difficulties. He was experiencing insomnia, loss of appetite, and feelings of hopelessness for approximately 3 weeks. Initial assessment revealed mild cognitive impairment consistent with age-related changes. Treatment included low-dose antidepressant medication (Sertraline 25mg) and counseling sessions. Patient showed significant improvement in mood and sleep patterns over 10-day hospitalization. Discharge medications include Sertraline 25mg once daily and low-dose Lorazepam for breakthrough anxiety. Family education provided regarding medication adherence and warning signs requiring immediate medical attention. Patient advised to follow up with Dr. Deshmukh at outpatient clinic in 2 weeks. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Savitri Patil]] was present for counseling and agrees to support medication compliance.

[[DISTRICT|Hingoli]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9423876541]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|ramesh.patil@abdm]]
[[CASTE|SC]]

payload.professional_persona: Dorje Bodhi, a seasoned call‑centre operator with decades of experience, combines a calm, empathetic voice, sharp active‑listening, and meticulous data‑entry skills to resolve caller issues efficiently while quietly mentoring newer agents, reflecting his methodical reliability and deep compassion.
payload.linguistic_persona: Dorje Bodhi, whose mother tongue is Marathi, navigates daily conversations fluently while relying on colleagues for any written tasks, as he remains illiterate in Marathi, Hindi, and English yet adept at spoken communication and visual computer cues.
payload.sports_persona: Dorje Bodhi enjoys morning walks through the countryside, follows cricket passionately, cheering for the Mumbai Indians, and occasionally joins the village’s informal kabaddi matches, though his age limits intense play.
payload.arts_persona: Dorje Bodhi is an avid fan of Marathi folk music, regularly listening to Avadhoot Gupte’s bhavgeet and the classic tunes of Lata Mangeshkar, and he delights in watching Tamasha performances featuring shahir Shahir Sable, often humming the melodies afterwards.
payload.travel_persona: Dorje Bodhi prefers carefully planned short trips with his wife and grandchildren, cherishing visits to nearby pilgrimage sites like Shirdi and Ajanta, the coastal breezes of Goa, and occasional pilgrimages to Bodh Gaya, while dreaming of a serene retreat in the Himalayan foothills.
payload.culinary_persona: Dorje Bodhi delights in savoring traditional Marathi dishes such as Puran Poli, Misal Pav, and Solkadhi, prefers fresh garden herbs like coriander and fenugreek for his tea, and while he enjoys buying street‑food snacks like vada pav, he rarely cooks because he relies on his wife’s kitchen.
payload.persona: Dorje Bodhi is a compassionate, methodical call‑centre veteran who balances frugality, community devotion, and a love for simple village pleasures, though he sometimes worries over health and finances.
payload.cultural_background: Dorje Bodhi belongs to a close‑knit rural Marathi community in Hingoli, Maharashtra. The agrarian rhythms, seasonal festivals, and the spirit of ‘jugaad’ shape his everyday life. As a convert to Navayana (Ambedkarite) Buddhism, his cultural identity intertwines traditional Maharashtrian customs—such as Ganesh Chaturthi, folk music, and communal meals—with Buddhist values of compassion, equality, and meditation. The village’s oral storytelling traditions and the collective harmony of its residents reinforce his compassionate and cooperative nature.
payload.linguistic_background: Dorje’s mother tongue is Marathi, which he speaks fluently and uses for all personal and work interactions. Because he never received formal schooling, he is illiterate in Marathi, Hindi, and English, relying on oral communication and on colleagues for any written tasks. He has no functional knowledge of English or any other language. In the call centre, he primarily handles Marathi‑speaking customers, using spoken language and basic computer navigation guided by visual cues.
payload.skills_and_expertise: With decades of experience as a call‑centre operator, Dorje has honed strong customer‑service skills, including calm voice handling, active listening, and rapid problem identification. His methodical approach ensures accurate data entry and adherence to scripts, while his empathetic nature aids in conflict resolution and de‑escalation of upset callers. He is proficient in basic computer operations, call‑logging software, and time‑management techniques. His collaborative spirit makes him an effective team player and mentor for newer agents.
payload.skills_and_expertise_list: ["Customer service", "Phone communication", "Active listening", "Problem solving", "Empathy", "Organizational planning", "Basic computer operation", "Time management", "Team coordination", "Conflict resolution"]
payload.hobbies_and_interests: Outside of work, Dorje enjoys listening to Buddhist talks and chants, attending village community gatherings, and walking through the countryside after his shift. He finds peace in gardening small pots of vegetables and herbs, and often plays card games like ‘Teen Patti’ with friends at the local tea stall. He loves Marathi folk music and occasionally joins local Tamasha performances as an enthusiastic audience member. Regular meditation sessions help him manage stress and maintain his compassionate outlook.
payload.hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Buddhist talks and chants", "Attending community gatherings", "Walking in the countryside", "Gardening", "Playing card games (Teen Patti)", "Listening to Marathi folk music", "Enjoying local Tamasha performances", "Meditation"]
payload.career_goals_and_ambitions: Dorje wishes to remain a dependable presence in his call‑centre for the next few years, gradually taking on a mentorship role where he can share his extensive experience with younger agents. He aims to secure a pension and sufficient savings to support his wife and grandchildren, while also planning a modest post‑retirement venture such as a small vegetable stall. Spiritually, he aspires to deepen his meditation practice and occasionally lead brief meditation sessions for community members, contributing to local welfare initiatives that promote health, education, and social harmony.
payload.persona_clinical_summary: A 67-year-old male operator in a call centre, currently married, with no formal education and a rural background in Maharashtra’s Hingoli district. His first language is Marathi, and he is illiterate in all languages. He lives in a close-knit rural community and is concerned about health and finances.

payload.generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-HNG-001]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Hingoli District Civil Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sunil Deshmukh]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Patil]], [[AGE|67]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Hingoli]]
Reason: Uncontrolled hypertension with associated symptoms.

Dear Sir/Madam,

This is to refer our patient, Mr. Ramesh Patil, a 67-year-old male from Hingoli, for specialist evaluation.

Mr. Patil has a known history of hypertension for over a decade. He is currently on Amlodipine 5mg and Telmisartan 40mg daily. However, he reports that his home blood pressure readings have been consistently high, often exceeding 180/110 mmHg, despite medication. He complains of frequent headaches, particularly in the mornings, and episodes of dizziness, which he attributes to stress from his work as a call centre operator. Given his background and limited formal education, medication adherence is a concern.

On examination today, his blood pressure was recorded at 182/112 mmHg. He has mild pedal edema bilaterally. Fundoscopic examination shows Grade II hypertensive retinopathy.

Considering his resistant hypertension and the risk of end-organ damage, we request a detailed evaluation by your esteemed department. His primary contact for follow-up is his wife, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Savitri Patil]], whose phone number is [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. For identification purposes, his [[MRN|MRN-HNG-2024-0042]], [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P8765432]], and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|MH01 20100012345]] are provided.

We would be grateful for your expert opinion and management plan.

Thank you.

Sincerely,
Dr. Sunil Deshmukh
General Medicine
Hingoli District Civil Hospital
